Your Majesty, the continuing conflict in the Gulf constitutes a significant problem for us as well as for the moderate Arab states of the region.

Our first desire is to see this agonizing war ended through immediate implementation of United Nations Security Council Resolution 598. The United States cannot stand by, however, and watch innocent shipping subjected to lawless attacks when we have the means to assist.

THE OPTIONS Our longstanding policy has been to support the OAU ’s peace keeping activities by urging the Moroccans and Algerians to be cooperative and the OAU to be energetic.
We have made the point that admission of the SDAR to the OAU would be inconsistent with the ceasefire/referendum the OAU itself has repeatedly said should determine the status of the Western Sahara.
Since the origin of the dispute, we have supported the principle of self-determination.
